Roadmap

Information on DirectX Audio is presented in the following sections:

  • Xbox Audio Hardware. Discussion of the capabilities and architecture of the Xbox Media Communications Processor (MCP).
  • Soundtrack Support on Xbox. Provides an overview of the low level API support for soundtracks and high level support using the Xbox Audio Creation Tool (XACT).
  • 3D Sound. Covers information on 3D sound on the Xbox.
  • Xbox Audio Creation Tool (XACT). Contains information related to the XACT tools and library. This area contains the following sections:
    • XACT Overview. Provides a general overview of features supported by the XACT engine. This section should be reviewed in conjunction with the XACT Concepts by both Sound Designers and Programmers.
    • XACT Concepts. Contains detailed definitions of concepts used throughout XACT. This section should be reviewed in conjunction with the XACT Overview by both Sound Designers and Programmers.
    • XACT Library. Detailed information covering all the library elements declared in the XACT header files.
  • DirectSound Library. Contains information related to the DirectSound library. This area contains the following sections:
    • Using DirectMusic with Xbox. Contrasts the differences between Microsoft® DirectMusic® in DirectX 8.0 and DirectMusic in DirectX for the Xbox.
    • Using DirectSound. A guide to using the DirectSound library. You'll probably want to familiarize yourself with the table of contents for this section, and then refer to parts of it as you need specific information. Use it in conjunction with the reference section.
    • DirectSound C/C++ Reference. Detailed information about all the library elements declared in dsound.h.
  • DirectMusic Library. Contains information related to the DirectMusic library. This area includes the following sections:
    • Using DirectSound with Xbox. Contrasts the differences between Microsoft® DirectSound® in DirectX 8.0 and DirectSound in DirectX for the Xbox.
    • Introduction to DirectMusic. An overview of what DirectMusic is and what it can do for your application, together with a first look at some important objects and the steps involved in playing audio.
    • Understanding DirectX Audio. A deeper look at the underlying mechanisms. This section will not teach you how to implement audio playback or capture in your application, but it will help you understand the libraries.
    • Using DirectMusic. A guide to using the DirectMusic library. You'll probably want to familiarize yourself with the table of contents for this section, and then refer to parts of it as you need specific information. Use it in conjunction with the reference section.
    • Advanced Topics in DirectMusic. Information of interest mostly for developing specialized applications or applications that need highly optimized performance.
    • DirectMusic C/C++ Reference. Detailed information about all the library elements declared in the DirectMusic header files. This section includes information about the file format used by DirectMusic and DirectMusic Producer.

DirectMusic Producer

DirectMusic Producer is a powerful authoring application included with the DirectX SDK. It enables composers to create dynamic musical elements that can be incorporated in an application.

If your focus is more on sound design than on application development, you might want to start with DirectMusic Producer Help, which is installed with that application.

If you are an application developer and just want an overview of the material you'll be working with, see Advanced Compositional Music Elements.

DirectMusic Audio Scripting

DirectMusic Audio Scripting is a feature used by composers and sound designers for more direct control over the soundtrack in an application. Documentation for the script API is included in the DirectMusic Producer Help.

For an overview, see Audio Scripts. To learn how to incorporate scripts in an application, see Using Audio Scripts.

Unpublished work. © 2000 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
